title: uniface.datastore
text: uniface.datastore The uniface.datastore API provides access to the local browser-based database used for client-side data storage. Functions Table 1. Datastore Functions Function Description put() Creates or updates a JSON document in a client-side database. get() Retrieves the specified JSON object from a client-side database. remove() Deletes the specified JSON document from a client-side database. Promises and Errors All of these functions return a JavaScript Promise, such as the specified JSON document or a response. If the Promise cannot be fulfilled, an error or exception is returned. The most common errors are: 409 conflict errors are returned when the database is unable to commit an action such as a put() or remove() , for example, a JSON document already exists with a specified _id , or when a specified _rev value does not match. 404 not found exceptions, which are returned when an attempt is made to retrieve data that does not exist. Such an exception might look like this: Explain this code { status: 404, name: "not_found", message: "missing", error: true, reason: "missing", } Copy You should always provide error handling for errors and exceptions. For example: Explain this code var myDoc = { _id: 'someid', _rev: '1-somerev' }; uniface.datastore.put(myDoc).then(function () { // success }).catch(function (err) { if (err.name === 'conflict') { // conflict! } else { // some other error } }); Copy Related Links Client-Side Data Storage Uniface dynamic server pages support client-side storage of data in a browser-based database, which makes it possible to save data locally when there is no connection to the server. This can be useful when you want to display read-only information that has previously been downloaded, or to save modified data until it can be synchronized or uploaded to the server. Promises A promise is a placeholder for the return value of an operation that has been called asynchronously. It enables you to specify subsequent actions to be performed with that value when it is eventually received. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
